title: Connecting the World: The Race for Internet Access

hero.title: Connecting the World: The Race for Internet Access
hero.lead: Internet use has soared, yet large gaps remain between cities and rural areas — and between income groups. Scroll to explore the urban–rural digital divide.

blocks.title: The urban–rural digital divide
blocks.subtitle: Individuals using the Internet, by place of residence (% of population)

scenes.text: Across every income group, urban residents are more likely to use the Internet than people in rural areas. The gap is widest in low-income economies.

blocks.html: Income shapes connectivity. High-income economies already show near-universal urban and rural use, while low-income countries still face double-digit gaps — and much lower absolute levels of access.

blocks.title: Internet use is rising — but not equally
blocks.subtitle: Individuals using the Internet (% of population)

scenes.text: In 2015, many countries still had low Internet use. Dots show each country’s starting point.
By 2024 most countries moved right — higher access — but the distance covered varies widely.
Color shows the speed of progress between 2015 and 2024.
Explore all countries. Highlight any economy to compare its path.
